channel.title: Home on ML Lab
channel.description: Recent content in Home on ML Lab

item.title: 1. Matplotlib
item.description: 1. Several Options import matplotlib as mpl ; mpl.rcParams['axes.unicode_minus'] = False import matplotlib.pyplot as plt ; plt.rcParams['font.family'] = 'AppleGothic' мңҲлҸ„мҡ° мң м Җлқјл©ҙ мІ« лІҲм§ё мӨ„мқҖ мһ…л Ҙн• н•„мҡ”к°Җ м—ҶлӢӨ. л§Ҙмқҳ кІҪмҡ° ‘-’ кё°нҳёк°Җ к№Ём ём„ң лӮҳнғҖлӮҳлҠ” кІҪмҡ°лҘј н•ҙкІ°н•ҳкё° мң„н•ҙ мІ« лІҲм§ё мӨ„мқҳ мҪ”

item.title: KBO м •к·ңмӢңмҰҢ нҢҖлі„ мҠ№лҘ , нғҖмңЁ л°Ҹ л°©м–ҙмңЁ(нҸүк· мһҗмұ…м җ) мҳҲмёЎ
2. Seaborn
item.description: 0. Import Seaborn and Datasets import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt ; plt.rcParams['font.family']='AppleGothic' ; plt.rc('axes', unicode_minus=False) import seaborn as sns Seabornмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкё° мң„н•ҙм„ңлҠ” matplotlibмқ„ лЁјм Җ importн•ҙм•ј н•ңлӢӨ. л‘җ лІҲм§ё мӨ„мқҳ мҳөм…ҳл“Ө

item.title: 3. Bokeh
item.description: 1. Introduction to Bokeh from bokeh.io import output_notebook, show from bokeh.plotting import figure мҡ°м„ мң„мқҳ лӘ…л №м–ҙлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳм—¬ BokehлҘј importн•ңлӢӨ. output_notebook() кё°ліём ҒмңјлЎң BokehлҠ” html нҳ•мӢқмқҳ кІ°кіјл¬јмқ„ мӮ°м¶ңн•ҳм§Җл§Ң, output_notebook() лӘ…л №м–ҙ

item.title: Gauss-Markov Theorem
item.description: Gauss-Markov TheoremмқҖ Least Square Estimationмқҙ к°ҖмһҘ мўӢмқҖ м¶”м •лҹүмһ„мқ„ мҰқлӘ…н•ҳлҠ” мқҙлЎ мқҙлӢӨ. мқҙлҘј BLUE(Best Linear Unbiased Estimator)лқјкі н‘ңнҳ„н•ҳкё°лҸ„ н•ңлӢӨ. мҡ°м„ мҡ°лҰ¬лҠ”

item.title: Linear Regression
item.description: Linear RegressionмқҖ Inputкіј Output мӮ¬мқҙмқҳ Linear Relationshipмқ„ к°Җм •н•ңлӢӨ. $$y=X\beta+\epsilon, \quad \epsilon \sim (0, \sigma^2I)$$ $$Y=\begin{bmatrix} y_1 \\ y_2 \\ \vdots \\ y_n \end{bmatrix} \qquad X=\begin{bmatrix} 1 & x_{11} & x_{12} & \cdots & x_{1p} \\ 1 &

item.title: QR-Decomposition
item.description: 1. Gram-Schmidt Process лӘЁл“ $n$м°Ёмӣҗ кіөк°„мқҳ мҳҒкіөк°„мқҙ м•„лӢҢ SubspaceлҠ” Orthonormal BasisлҘј к°–лҠ”лӢӨ. Gram-Schmidt ProcessлҠ” $n$м°Ёмӣҗ кіөк°„мқҳ мҳҒкіөк°„мқҙ м•„лӢҢ Subspace

item.title: Normal Model
item.description: 3.1. Inference for the Mean, Conditional on the Variance 3.1.1. Posterior Inference Sampling Model: $Y_1, \ldots, Y_n|\theta, \sigma^2 \sim N(\theta, \sigma^2)$ Prior Distribution: $\theta|\sigma^2 \sim N(\mu_0, \tau_0^2)$ $\begin{aligned} p(\theta|y_1, \ldots, y_n, \sigma^2) &\propto p(y_1, \ldots, y_n|\theta, \sigma^2) \times p(\theta|\sigma^2) \\ &\propto \exp{\left\{-\dfrac{\sum(y_i-\theta)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right\}}\times\exp{\left\{-\dfrac{(\theta-\mu_0)^2}{2\tau_0^2}\right\}} \\ &\propto \exp{\left[-\dfrac{1}{2}\left\{\left(\dfrac{1}{\tau_0^2}+\dfrac{n}{\sigma^2}\right)\theta^2+2\left(\dfrac{\mu_0}{\tau_0^2}+\dfrac{\sum y_i}{\sigma^2}\right)\theta\right\}\right]} \\ &\propto \exp{\left\{-\dfrac{1}{2}(a\theta^2+2b\theta)\right\}} \\ &=\exp{\left\{-\dfrac{1}{2}a\left(\theta^2-\dfrac{2b}{a}\theta+\dfrac{b^2}{a^2}\right)+\dfrac{b^2}{2a}\right\}} \\ &\propto \exp{\left\{-\dfrac{1}{2}a\left(\theta-\dfrac{b}{a}\right)^2\right\}} \\ &=\exp{\left\{-\dfrac{1}{2}\left(\dfrac{\theta-b/a}{1/\sqrt{a}}\right)^2\right\}} \sim N\left(\dfrac{b}{a}, \dfrac{1}{a}\right)=N(\mu_n, \tau_n^2) \end{aligned}$ 3.1.2. Parameter Update $\mu_0\rightarrow\mu_n=\dfrac{\frac{1}{\tau_0^2}\times\mu_0+\frac{n}{\sigma^2}\times\bar{y}}{\frac{1}{\tau_0^2}+\frac{n}{\sigma^2}}=\dfrac{\kappa_0\mu_0+n\bar{y}}{\kappa_0+n}=\dfrac{\kappa_0\mu_0+n\bar{y}}{\kappa_n}$ Posterior Mean $\m

item.title: One-parameter models
item.description: 2.1. The Binomial Model 2.1.1. Inference for Exchangable Binary Data Posterior Inference Under a Unifrom Prior Distribution $Y_i$к°Җ нҸүк· мқҙ $\theta$мқё i.i.d. Binary Variableмқё кІҪмҡ° Sampling ModelмқҖ $p(y_1, \ldots, y_n|\theta)=\theta^{\sum y_i}(1-\theta)^{n-\sum y_i}$мҷҖ к°ҷлӢӨ. мқҙл•Ң

item.title: Bayesian Statistics
item.description: 1.1. Introduction Bayesian Learning нҶөкі„м Ғ м¶”лЎ мқҖ лӘЁм§‘лӢЁмқҳ мқјл¶ҖлҘј нҶөн•ҙ лӘЁм§‘лӢЁмқҳ мқјл°ҳм Ғмқё нҠ№м„ұмқ„ м•Ңм•„лӮҙкё° мң„н•ң кіјм •мқҙлӢӨ. мқҙл•Ң лҢҖл¶Җл¶„мқҳ кІҪмҡ° лӘЁм§‘лӢЁмқҳ мҲҳм№ҳм Ғ нҠ№м„ұмқ„ лӘЁмҲҳ $\theta$лЎң

item.title: Moving Beyond Linearity
item.description: м§ҖкёҲк№Ңм§ҖлҠ” м„ нҳ• лӘЁнҳ•м—җ мҙҲм җмқ„ л§һм¶”м–ҙ мҷ”лӢӨ. 6мһҘм—җм„ң Ridge, Lasso, PCA л“ұ м„ нҳ• лӘЁнҳ•мқҳ ліөмһЎм„ұмқ„ мӨ„мқҙлҠ” л°©лІ•л“Өмқ„ лӢӨлЈЁкёҙ н•ҳмҳҖм§Җл§Ң, мқҙ м—ӯмӢң кІ°көӯ м„ нҳ• лӘЁнҳ•мқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳлҠ” кІғмқҙм—ҲлӢӨ.

item.title: Linear Model Selection and Regularization
item.description: м„ нҳ• лӘЁнҳ•мқҖ л§ҺмқҖ м „м ңмЎ°кұҙмқ„ н•„мҡ”лЎң н•ҳм§Җл§Ң, м¶”лЎ мҳҒм—ӯм—җм„ң к°–лҠ” к°•м җмңјлЎң мқён•ҙ мӢӨмғқнҷңмқҳ л¬ём ңл“Өмқ„ н‘ёлҠ”лҚ° мһҲм–ҙ л№„м„ нҳ• лӘЁнҳ•м—җ лҢҖн•ҙ кІҪмҹҒл Ҙмқ„ к°Җм§Җкі мһҲлӢӨ. 6мһҘм—җм„ңлҠ” Method

item.title: Resampling Methods
item.description: Resampling MethodsлҠ” Training DataлЎңл¶Җн„° л°ҳліөм ҒмңјлЎң н‘ңліёмқ„ м¶”м¶ңн•ҳкі , м¶”м¶ңн•ң н‘ңліёмқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳм—¬ м—¬лҹ¬ лІҲ лӘЁнҳ•мқ„ м Ғн•©н•ҳм—¬ н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ лӘЁнҳ•мқ„ м Ғн•©н• л•ҢлҠ” м–»мқ„ мҲҳ м—ҶлҠ” м •ліҙлҘј м–»

item.title: Linear Regression
item.description: 3.1. Simple Linear Regression Simple Linear RegressionмқҖ ліҖмҲҳ к°„мқҳ м„ нҳ• кҙҖкі„лҘј мһҳ лӮҳнғҖлӮҙлҠ” мөңм Ғмқҳ м§Ғм„ $\beta_0+\beta_1X$лҘј м°ҫлҠ” мһ‘м—…мқҙлӢӨ. м§Ғм„ мқҳ мӢқмқ„ м•„лҠ” кІҪ

item.title: About Me
item.description: Profile м—°м„ёлҢҖн•ҷкөҗм—җм„ң мқ‘мҡ©нҶөкі„н•ҷмқ„ м „кіөн•ҳмҳҖмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. (2019.08 ~ 2021.02) м—°м„ёлҢҖн•ҷкөҗ мқ‘мҡ©нҶөкі„н•ҷкіј кіөмӢқ мқёмҰқ нҶөкі„ л°Ҹ лҚ°мқҙн„°мӮ¬мқҙм–ёмҠӨ н•ҷнҡҢ ESCм—җм„ң нҷңлҸҷн•ҳмҳҖмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ. (2019.09 ~ 2020.08) || Github

item.title: Classification
item.description: 4мһҘм—җм„ңлҠ” л°ҳмқ‘ліҖмҲҳк°Җ лІ”мЈјнҳ•мқё кІҪмҡ°, мҰү, Classificationмқҳ л¬ём ңлҘј лӢӨлЈ¬лӢӨ. 4.2. Why Not Linear Regression? м„ нҳ• нҡҢк·Җ лӘЁнҳ•мқҖ л°ҳмқ‘ліҖмҲҳк°Җ лІ”мЈјнҳ•мқё кІҪмҡ° мӮ¬мҡ©мқҙ м Ғн•©н•ҳ

item.title: Statistical Learning
item.description: 2.1. What is Statistical Learning? 2.1.1. Why Estimate $f$? мҡ°лҰ¬к°Җ $f$лҘј м¶”м •н•ҳлҠ” лӘ©м ҒмқҖ мҳҲмёЎкіј н•ҙм„қмқ„ н•ҳкё° мң„н•ЁмқҙлӢӨ. $$\hat{Y}=\hat{f}(X)$$ 1. мҳҲмёЎ $f$мқҳ м¶”м •м№ҳ $\hat{f}$лҘј м•ҲлӢӨл©ҙ, мҡ°лҰ¬к°Җ к°Җм§„ $X_1, X_2, …, X

item.title: MyLDA.py
item.description: Github Link: MyLDA.ipynb import numpy as np class MyLDA: def fit(self, X_train, y_train): X_train = np.array(X_train) y_train = np.array(y_train) self.pi = [len(X_train[y_train==i])/len(X_train) for i in np.unique(y_train)] self.mu = [np.sum(X_train[y_train==i], axis=0)/len(X_train[y_train==i]) for i in np.unique(y_train)] self.sigma = np.sum([(np.transpose(X_train[y_train==i]-self.mu[i])).dot(X_train[y_train==i]-self.mu[i]) for i in np.unique(y_train)], axis=0)/(len(X_train)-len(np.unique(y_train))) def predict(self, X_test): delta = [X_test.dot(np.linalg.inv(self.sigma)).dot(self.mu[i])-0.5*self.mu[i].dot(np.linalg.inv(self.sigma)).dot(self.mu[i])+np.log(self.pi[i]) for i in np.unique(y)] yhat = np.argmax(delta, axis=0) return yhat

item.title: MyLogisticRegression.py
item.description: Github Link: MyLogisticRegression.ipynb import numpy as np class MyLogisticRegression: def __init__(self, max_iter=10): self.max_iter = max_iter def fit(self, X_train, y_train): ones = np.transpose(np.array([[1]*len(X_train)])) X_train = np.concatenate((ones, np.array(X_train)), axis=1) y_train = np.array(y_train) beta = np.array([0]*X_train.shape[1]) for i in range(self.max_iter): p1 = np.exp(X_train.dot(beta))/(1+np.exp(X_train.dot(beta))) p0 = 1-p1 W = np.diag(p0*p1) beta = beta + np.linalg.inv(np.transpose(X_train).dot(W).dot(X_train)).dot(np.transpose(X_train)).dot(y_train-p1) self.beta_new = beta def predict(self, X_test): ones = np.transpose(np.array([[1]*len(X_test)])) X_test = np.concatenate((ones, np.array(X_test)), axis=1) return (np.exp(X_test.dot(self.beta_new))/(1+np.exp(X_test.dot(self.beta_new))) > 0.5).astype('int')

item.title: MyNaturalCubicSpline.py
item.description: Github Link: MyNaturalCubicSpline.ipynb import numpy as np class MyNaturalCubicSpline: def fit(self, X_train, y_train, k): X_train = np.array(X_train) y_train = np.array(y_train) self.k = np.array(k) X_train_new = np.column_stack((np.ones(len(X_train)), X_train)) d_Km1 = (np.where(np.power(X_train-k[-2], 3) < 0, 0, np.power(X_train-k[-2], 3))-np.where(np.power(X_train-k[-1], 3) < 0, 0, np.power(X_train-k[-1], 3)))/(k[-2]-k[-1]) for i in range(len(k)-2): d = (np.where(np.power(X_train-k[i], 3) < 0, 0, np.power(X_train-k[i], 3))-np.where(np.power(X_train-k[-1], 3) < 0, 0, np.power(X_train-k[-1], 3)))/(k[i]-k[-1]) X_train_new = np.column_stack((X_train_new, d-d_Km1)) self.beta = np.linalg.inv(np.transpose(X_train_new).dot(X_train_new)).dot(np.transpose(X_train_new)).dot(y_train) def predict(self, X_test): X_test = np.

item.title: MyRegressionSpline.py
item.description: Github Link: MyRegressionSpline.ipynb import numpy as np class MyRegressionSpline: def fit(self, X_train, y_train, m, k): X_train = np.array(X_train) y_train = np.array(y_train) self.m = m self.k = np.array(k) X_train_new = np.power(X_train, 0) for i in range(1, m+1): X_train_new = np.column_stack((X_train_new, np.power(X_train, i))) for i in range(len(k)): X_train_new = np.column_stack((X_train_new, np.where(np.power(X_train-k[i], m) < 0, 0, np.power(X_train-k[i], m)))) self.beta = np.linalg.inv(np.transpose(X_train_new).dot(X_train_new)).dot(np.transpose(X_train_new)).dot(y_train) def predict(self, X_test): X_test = np.array(X_test) X_test_new = np.power(X_test, 0) for i in range(1, self.
